ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code exists specifically for dacquoise; A0CJW (Pastries and cakes) is the closest defensible broader category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for dacquoise or meringue-based pastry; left null to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One piece of pistachio and cream cheese dacquoise, approximately 80 g, as typically served in Singapore bakeries.

nutrient_source.title: Pistachio & Cream Cheese Dacquoise — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Pistachio & Cream Cheese Dacquoise (cr_id G00011026). Saturated fat not reported by HPB; estimated at 9.5 g/100g based on USDA FoodData Central cream cheese and butter fatty acid profiles proportional to ingredient composition. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and micronutrients beyond those reported by HPB are set to -1 (not available in source).

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — analogous estimate based on meringue/nut-based pastry with cream cheese filling
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for pistachio and cream cheese dacquoise. GI estimated at 45 (low) based on analogous meringue-based pastry with nut and dairy filling from the University of Sydney GI Database. The low carbohydrate content (8.85 g/100g), high protein (16.09 g/100g), and high fat (23.06 g/100g) strongly attenuate postprandial glucose response. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (45) × carbohydrate per serving (8.85 g/100g × 80 g serving = 7.08 g) / 100 = 3.2 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Extremely high sodium content at 1279 mg/100g, far exceeding the 600 mg/100g threshold for hypertension concern
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should strictly avoid this food; even a single 80 g serving delivers approximately 1023 mg sodium, approaching or exceeding the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg for hypertensive patients.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate (8.85 g/100g) and low estimated GI (~45) are favourable, but high saturated fat (~9.5 g/100g) and elevated sodium may worsen insulin resistance and cardiovascular risk over time
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with type 2 diabetes may consume this food in small portions (one piece, ~80 g) occasionally, as the low carbohydrate and high protein content moderate glycemic response, but should monitor total saturated fat intake and be aware of the very high sodium content.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (~9.5 g/100g from cream cheese and butter) combined with very high sodium (1279 mg/100g) and moderate cholesterol (78 mg/100g) present compounded cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit or avoid this food due to the combination of high saturated fat, very high sodium, and moderate cholesterol, all of which adversely affect lipid profiles and blood pressure management.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat (~9.5 g/100g) from cream cheese and butter exceeds the 5 g/100g RA threshold; zero dietary fibre provides no anti-inflammatory gut microbiome benefit
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid regular consumption of this food as the high saturated fat from cream cheese and butter is pro-inflammatory, and the absence of dietary fibre provides no compensatory gut microbiome benefit.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pistachio and Cream Cheese Dacquoise is a French-inspired meringue-based pastry sold as an individual piece in Singapore bakeries. A dacquoise is a layered confection built from baked nut-meringue discs — in this case flavoured with ground pistachio — sandwiched around a rich cream cheese filling. The exterior layers are crisp and airy from the whipped egg-white and nut base, while the interior is dense, tangy, and creamy from the cream cheese. The overall flavour profile is nutty, lightly sweet, and mildly savoury. Nutritionally, this item is notable for its high protein content (16.09 g/100g), which is unusually elevated for a pastry and likely reflects the cream cheese and egg white components. Fat is high at 23.06 g/100g, predominantly saturated fat estimated at approximately 9.5 g/100g from cream cheese and butter. Carbohydrate is low at 8.85 g/100g with 6.24 g sugar, making the glycemic impact modest. However, sodium is extremely high at 1279 mg/100g, which is clinically significant and disqualifying for hypertension management. Calcium is exceptionally high at 1375 mg/100g, reflecting the cream cheese content. This food is not suitable for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease due to its sodium and saturated fat load. It may be consumed in small portions by individuals managing type 2 diabetes given its low carbohydrate and high protein profile, but the saturated fat content warrants caution. It is not suitable for vegans, and contains common allergens including eggs, dairy, and tree nuts (pistachio).
common_user_queries: Is pistachio dacquoise suitable for diabetics?
How much sodium is in pistachio cream cheese dacquoise?
Can I eat dacquoise if I have high blood pressure?
What allergens are in pistachio dacquoise?
How many calories in one piece of dacquoise?
What is the glycemic index of dacquoise?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00011026, confidence 0.95). Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central cream cheese and butter fatty acid profiles as HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown; this is documented. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database as no direct measurement exists for this specific product.
notes: 1. dietary_fibre_g is reported as 0.0 by HPB; this is treated as confirmed zero per the HPB source for this product formulation. 2. saturated_fat_g (9.5 g/100g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central cream cheese (USDA FDC ID 173417) and butter fatty acid profiles, proportional to the ingredient composition of this dacquoise; not directly reported by HPB. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions and micronutrient data for this specific Singapore bakery dacquoise product are not available in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. 4. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate based on meringue/nut-based pastry with cream cheese filling from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for pistachio cream cheese dacquoise has been published. 5. Halal status not confirmed; cream cheese and butter sourcing not verified for halal certification. 6. The extremely high sodium (1279 mg/100g) is unusual for a sweet pastry and may reflect the HPB source data accurately capturing a specific commercial formulation; this value is preserved as reported.
